author: @jujitsu84asdf73
text: I love the TinkerCad tutorial!  It is so easy to use that sometimes it is harder, because it doesn’t make you fully define a sketch like professional CAD versions. Please consider doing more videos like this when building your projects. This video was a very helpful resource!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Glad it was helpful - I will try to do more of this kind of thing

author: @BlackOpsJuJu

author: @salimufari
text: 12:40 Can't you just take the hole, Copy it, enlarge that copy after swapping it to a solid to a hole. Then align the gear, hole & enlarged yoke. Merge the 3 & be done?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: you can do whatever you want mate as long as it works for you - this is a guide not a rule book

author: @scotttovey
text:
This basic design is the basis of a reciprocal saw.
A butter maker.

An EIM (extremely illogical machine), which pushes a button at either end of it's reach to reverse the motion of it's drive motor, thus creating a reciprocal motion.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: lol - I really like the EIM - it's in my mind lol

replies.author: @scotttovey
replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkering 
😎👍

author: @marjon1703
text: Thank you so much for taking the time to tutorial your designs. I've being using CAD packages since the dark old days of DOGS ['Nova CAD' ... I Hope]. I love Tinker-Cad for its logical and intuitive front-end, but hit its limitations constantly. Every tutorial you do I discover a new tool in Tinker-Cad I knew nothing of, as they are hidden away in rabbit warren options. Please keep these enjoyable deep dives coming!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I like it for that too - it makes it easy for folks to pick it up - but you are also right that some functionality isn't obvious - I am being kind to Tinkercad lol - which makes it hard for folks to get how useful it can be - I am glad you like the vid mate

author: @orpheuscreativeco9236
text: With a little flywheel that would make for a great sawing mechanism 🤔 Very cool build Rob! Thanks for sharing ✌️😃

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: good one mate - you are right it would!

author: @mikehen06

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Thank you for taking the time to say that - you are right she was really very lovely and made me a better person.

author: @knightscape
text: Awesome workflow demonstrations in tinkercad lately!  I’m working on making a push-push (technically a pull-pull) switch mechanism to operate a swaybar disconnect on my Jeep. (Offroad Only’s Swayloc for reference). I’ve been struggling through the tinkercad interface with very few good functional,  technical tutorials to follow.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Someone mentioned I didn't do enough tutorials on how i work - so I am trying to remedy that - glad it helps mate

replies.author: @knightscape
replies.text: @ in two videos, you’ve unlocked two projects I’ve been struggling with. Moving workplaces to surfaces and code blocks weren’t immediately obvious in their use within tinkercad. Being a dive in and discover type of learner, their use needs instruction to even create awareness of their existence.
